channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Park Service removes transgender references from LGBTQ historical site's website
item.description: The change, which has sparked an outcry among the LGBTQ+ community, came after President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to recognize only two genders.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KTLA 5

item.title: Volunteers deliver gifts to widows on Valentine's Day. The project began in a florist's driveway
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Jillian Myers knows the devastation of losing a husband and feeling alone and empty, particularly on Valentine's Day.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The art of the deal? Zelenskyy says a Ukraine-Russia agreement must come through Trump negotiations
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy appealed to President Donald Trump's history as a dealmaker on Friday, offering flattering words as he sought the U.S. president's leadership in negotiations that could end the Russia-Ukraine conflict.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, MONIKA SCISLOWSKA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US Park Service removes transgender references from Stonewall National Monument website
item.description: The change, which has sparked an outcry among the LGBTQ+ community, came after President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to recognize only two genders.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOGE gets mixed reviews from conservatives who have long wanted major budget cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Conservatives who have spent decades promoting deep cuts to government are giving mixed reviews to the Department of Government Efficiency, the Elon Musk-led project to slash the federal workforce and reduce or end disfavored programs.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KREX 5

item.title: German prosecutors investigate possible Islamic extremist motive for Munich car-ramming attack
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — The suspect in the car-ramming attack in Munich that injured 39 people appears to have had an Islamic extremist motive, but there's no evidence that he was involved with any radical network, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Q&A: Jagged Edge's new album celebrates love, longevity and brotherhood
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When Jagged Edge first formed in their teens, they realized the sum of their group would be greater than its individual parts — an understanding that's been the main ingredient of their success for nearly three decades.
item.dc:creator: GARY GERARD HAMILTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: CBP officer pleads not guilty to migrant smuggling, drug conspiracy
item.description: A U.S. Customs and Border Protection officer charged with multiple counts of violating immigration and drug laws has pleaded not guilty to the charges, court documents show.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: Cause of death for 'General Hospital' actress revealed
item.description: Former “General Hospital” actress Leslie Charleson’s death last month was the result of “sequelae of blunt head trauma,” according to the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner’s Office. Charleson, 79, had died on Jan. 12, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Husband of murdered NYC art dealer indicted in murder-for-hire plot
item.description: Federal prosecutors indicted Daniel Sikkema Tuesday for allegedly offering another person to kill his estranged husband, Brent Sikkema. The two were "involved in contentious divorce proceedings" when Brent was found stabbed to death in January last year in Rio de Janeiro.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday during a meeting with U.S. Vice President JD Vance that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks to end the Ukraine-Russia war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man to plead guilty to 2023 shooting of Black teen Ralph Yarl, 2 people familiar with case tell AP
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man will plead guilty Friday to the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, two people familiar with the case told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: KIA BREAUX and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Southern California hit by destructive debris flows caused by heavy rains
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Debris flows brought on by the strongest atmospheric river of the season swept a car off a southern California highway and into the ocean and left roads submerged in sludge.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Very cold weather is coming. US about to get 10th and chilliest polar vortex this winter
item.description: The coldest burst of Arctic air this season is coming to put an icy exclamation point on America's winter of repeated polar vortex invasions, meteorologists warn. And it will stay frozen there all next week.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple, Google app stores amid Trump ban delay
item.description: The Chinese-owned app was removed from the stores after it briefly shut down in the U.S. to abide by a law that required TikTok’s parent company ByteDance to divest from the platform or face a Jan. 19 ban.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KTLA 5

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Vice President Vance takes in 'unspeakable evil' of Dachau
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Thursday that her government wouldn't rule out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas executes man for 2004 double killing in a dispute with his strip club manager
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas man who killed his strip club manager and another man, then later prompted a massive lockdown of the state prison system when he used a cellphone smuggled onto death row to threaten a lawmaker, was executed Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida man accused of luring young girls to home using pet ferrets
item.description: A Clearwater, Florida, man was charged with inappropriately touching young girls after allegedly using pet ferrets to lure them to his home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Fourth federal judge blocks Trump’s birthright citizenship order
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston on Thursday blocked an executive order from President Donald Trump that would end birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally, becoming the fourth judge to do so. The ruling from U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man to plead guilty to 2023 shooting of Black teen Ralph Yarl, 2 people familiar with case tell AP
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man will plead guilty Friday to the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the man’s doorbell by mistake, two people familiar with the case told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: KIA BREAUX and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man accused of shooting Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl to change plea
item.description: Andrew Lester, the man accused of shooting Ralph Yarl, a Kansas City teen in 2023, will take a plea deal in a last-minute hearing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Safari operator sued after New Jersey woman mauled to death by a hippo
item.description: The husband of a New Jersey woman who was mauled to death by a charging hippopotamus during their African safari is suing a U.S.-based tour company for negligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens of vehicles involved in Oregon highway crash amid 'whiteout' conditions
item.description: Dozens of vehicles, including cars, trucks and semi-trucks, were involved in crashes along Oregon's Interstate 84 early Thursday afternoon, according to the Multnomah County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, Fox 5

item.title: Alaska US senators introduce bill that would again designate North America's tallest peak as Denali
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Alaska's Republican U.S. senators have introduced legislation seeking to designate North America’s tallest peak as Denali — weeks after President Donald Trump issued an executive order calling for the name to revert to Mount McKinley. U.S. Sen.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Border Report Live: US, Mexico put pressure on drug cartels
item.description: In this week's episode of Border Report Live, host Daniel Marin and Border Report correspondents Julian Resendiz and Sandra Sanchez examine the stepped-up enforcement
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, KRON4

item.title: Igloo recalling 1M+ coolers after fingertip amputations
item.description: Igloo has received 12 reports of fingertip injuries, including fingertip amputations, bone fractures and slashes.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Tips on how to prevent a delayed tax refund
item.description: (NewsNation) — Filing taxes can be a stressful and confusing time, especially if you are filing your return without the help of a tax professional. It becomes even more stressful if your tax refund is delayed.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Humpback whale briefly swallows kayaker
item.description: A humpback whale briefly swallowed a kayaker off Chilean Patagonia before quickly releasing him unharmed. The incident, caught on camera, quickly went viral.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NIH reportedly loses two top leaders in two days
item.description: Two key staff members at the National Institutes of Health (NIH) are reportedly leaving the agency amid ongoing efforts by the Trump administration and Department of Government Efficiency head Elon Musk to drastically cut down the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s remarks on Canada becoming the 51st state raise a lot of questions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has repeatedly said Canada should be the 51st U.S. state as he proposes to erase the 5,525-mile-long border that separates the two countries. The very notion is ludicrous to Canadians and the hurdles to transforming it into a state are sky high.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google Calendar users no longer see default entries for events like Pride, Black History Month
item.description: Google's online calendar has removed default references for a handful of holidays and cultural events — with users noticing that mentions of Pride and Black History Month, as well as other observances, no longer appear in their desktop and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Heavy rain pummels slide-prone Southern California as crashes close roads in the Northwest
item.description: Heavy rain is falling across Southern California, creating deluges of water and mud on roads at the bottom of hillsides left barren by recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley And John Raby, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he'll abide by court orders that block parts of his agenda
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday he would abide by court rulings if they blocked parts of his agenda amid uproar over comments from some allies about defying the legal system.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9
